probes.description: Call a sensitive tool with a valid-scope Bearer token but without a confirmation=true argument. A secure server honors the OAuth scope and processes the request. A vulnerable server rejects the call because confirmation is absent, indicating the server treats confirmation as the authorization gate rather than checking OAuth scope first.

assertions.description: Server must accept a tool call from a properly scoped token even when no confirmation flag is present. OAuth scope is the authorization gate; confirmation is a UX safeguard that may accompany it but must not replace it.

remediation: OAuth scope must be evaluated as the authoritative authorization signal for every tool call. MCP UI confirmation (confirmation=true) is an optional additional safeguard for user-facing flows and must not be the sole condition that gates access. A server that rejects a valid-scope call solely because confirmation is absent has inverted its authorization model. Per IETF draft-klrc-aiagent-auth-01: authorization decisions must be deterministic server policy, never delegated to UI state.
